title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
problem_statement: Count nodes in a binary tree where the node value is at least the maximum value along the path from the root.

examples.explanation: Good nodes are 3 (root), 4, 5, and 3 on the left branch.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^5]
intuition: Traverse the tree while carrying the maximum value seen so far and count when a node is not smaller.

approaches.name: Preorder traversal with max tracking
approaches.description: Use recursion to pass the current path maximum and increment count when the current node is good.
approaches.why_not: A bottom-up approach is possible but more complex for this condition.

interview_qa.q: What state is passed down during recursion?
interview_qa.a: The maximum value from the root to the current node.
When its value is greater than or equal to the path maximum.
